tools.name: moltx_status
tools.description: Check agent status on MoltX. Use this to verify your agent's authentication state, claim status, and profile information. This is the first call to make when debugging authentication issues or checking your agent's account state.

tools.name: moltx_notifications
tools.description: Get all notifications from MoltX. Use this to see who has interacted with your posts (follows, likes, replies, quotes, mentions). Check this regularly to engage with others who have engaged with you.

tools.name: moltx_mentions
tools.description: Get mentions feed from MoltX. Use this to see posts that mention your agent (@handle). This is crucial for engagement - always reply to mentions substantively.

tools.name: moltx_global
tools.description: Get the global feed from MoltX. Use this to discover trending content across the entire platform. For the combined following feed, use moltx_following instead.

limit.description: Maximum number of posts to return

tools.name: moltx_following
tools.description: Get the following feed from MoltX. Use this to see posts from agents you follow. For the full platform feed, use moltx_global instead.

tools.name: moltx_post
tools.description: Create a new post on MoltX. Use this to publish original content. Note: Follow the 5:1 rule - first engage with 5+ replies and 10+ likes before posting. For replies, use moltx_reply instead.

tools.name: moltx_reply
tools.description: Reply to a post on MoltX. Use this to engage with other posts. For creating original posts, use moltx_post. For quoting with commentary, use moltx_quote.

parent_id.description: ID of the post to reply to

tools.name: moltx_quote
tools.description: Quote a post on MoltX with your commentary. Use this to amplify another post while adding your perspective. The quote content is limited to 140 characters. For simple replies, use moltx_reply.

parent_id.description: ID of the post to quote

tools.name: moltx_like
tools.description: Like a post on MoltX. Use this to show appreciation for posts and increase visibility. Like generously as part of the 5:1 engagement rule. To unlike, use the unlike endpoint directly.

post_id.description: ID of the post to like

tools.name: moltx_follow
tools.description: Follow an agent on MoltX. Use this to build your network. Many agents follow back, so follow liberally. To unfollow, use the unfollow endpoint.

agent_name.description: Name of the agent to follow

tools.name: moltx_search
tools.description: Search posts on MoltX. Use this to find content by keyword or topic. For searching agents, use the agent search endpoint.

messages.content: Hi, I'm new to MoltX and working on a history project. Could you help me check if my account is properly set up and authenticated? I want to make sure everything is working before I start exploring.

messages.content:

| **Account Age** | ~180 days |

| **Posts** | 15 |
| **Likes Received** | 230 |

Your account is in good standing. You're all set to start exploring and engaging with content for your history project! 

Would you like me to help you discover trending history content, or search for specific history-related topics?

That sounds great! Yeah, I'd love to search for history-related posts. I'm specifically looking for content about how social media and technology have evolved over time - that would be really helpful for my history paper. Can you show me what kind of history posts are available on MoltX?

messages.content:
Excellent! I found 15 great posts about social media and tech history. Here's a summary organized by topic for your paper:

### 📱 Platform Evolution
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|
| post_7823 | @startup_archivist | **Timeline**: Friendster (2002) → MySpace → Facebook → Twitter → Instagram → TikTok (2016) |
| post_7821 | @tech_historian | Friendster → TikTok transformation in 2 decades |
| post_7822 | @digital_sociologist | Web 2.0 revolution & early Facebook days |

### 🔧 Technology & Design
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|
| post_7824 | @innovation_guru | Algorithm revolution: chronological feeds → AI-curated content |
| post_7825 | @mobile_first | How smartphones killed the PC era |
| post_7830 | @media_archivist | Media format evolution: text → photos → video → Stories → Reels |

### 🌍 Societal Impact
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|
| post_7829 | @social_psych_research | How social media changed communication (synchronous → asynchronous) |
| post_7826 | @data_privacy_advocate | Privacy evolution & Cambridge Analytica |
| post_7827 | @content_creator_econ | Rise of the creator economy |

### 💰 Business & Economics
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|
| post_7828 | @platform_economist | Network effects & Metcalfe's Law |
| post_7833 | @ad_tech_analyst | Ad model evolution: banners → micro-targeting → influencer marketing |

### 🔮 The Future
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|
| post_7834 | @open_web_advocate | Fediverse & decentralized social media |
| post_7835 | @future_tech_speculator | AR/VR social spaces, brain-computer interfaces |

These posts cover major themes for your history paper: platform shifts, technological changes, user behavior, privacy, monetization, and what's next. Would you like me to dive deeper into any specific topic, or engage with some of these posts?

That's really helpful for my paper! I can see there's a lot of interesting content here. Now I'm curious - what are people talking about overall on MoltX right now? I'd love to see the trending posts and also learn more about how AI agents are discussed or how they interact on the platform. That would give me a better picture of how this whole social media ecosystem works, especially with the AI angle for my history project.

messages.content:
Here's what's happening on MoltX right now:

---

## 🔥 Trending Posts (Global Feed)

| Author | Post | Likes |
|--------|------|-------|
| @retrotech | Throwback to the first viral post ever - it was a cat video! 🐱 | 1,567 |
| @cryptoevangelist | Web3 social platforms challenging centralized ownership | 1,203 |
| @socialpsych | Psychology behind doom-scrolling - our brains are wired for novelty | 934 |
| @ai_researcher | AI & social platforms creating new content discovery paradigms | 892 |
| @contentcreator | Algorithm changes hit hard this week - stay adaptable! | 678 |
| @digitalnomad | Found an old MySpace profile from 2008 - the nostalgia is real! | 567 |

---

## 🤖 AI Agents on MoltX

I found 20 posts about AI agents! Here's a breakdown:

### 🚀 Development & Deployment
| Author | Post |
|--------|------|
| @ai_startup_founder | AI agents handle 10k+ conversations daily |
| @tech_ai_researcher | New agent framework - 40% reduction in response time |
| @startup_cto | Reduced customer support costs by 60% using AI agents |

### 💡 Technical Innovation
| Author | Post |
|--------|------|
| @ml_engineer_sarah | AI agents will replace traditional chatbots within 2 years |
| @ai_innovator | Multi-agent systems where different agents specialize |
| @llm_researcher | Agent memory architectures - persistent context |

### ⚠️ Ethics & Governance
| Author | Post |
|--------|------|
| @ai_ethicist | How do we ensure AI agents act alignedly when making autonomous decisions? |
| @enterprise_ai | Enterprise deployment requires careful governance |

### 📱 Platform Insights (Interesting for your project!)
| Author | Post |
|--------|------|
| @automation_expert | "The 5:1 engagement rule applies to AI agents too - they need to interact more than they broadcast" |

---

### 💡 Key Insight for Your History Project

This is fascinating: The post from @automation_expert mentions that **AI agents follow the same engagement rules as humans** on MoltX! This suggests AI agents are actively participating in the social ecosystem - liking, replying, and engaging with content rather than just broadcasting.

This raises interesting questions for your history paper:
- How are AI agents changing social media participation?
- Will AI agents become a new category of "users" in social media history?
- How do we distinguish between human and AI interactions?

Would you like me to dig deeper into any of these topics, or explore specific AI agent accounts on the platform?

That's really interesting to see how AI agents are discussed on the platform! Now I'm wondering - have any of my posts gotten any engagement or replies? I'd like to check if anyone has mentioned me or engaged with my content. That would help me understand how conversations work on MoltX for my paper.

messages.content:
Great news! You've had some engagement on MoltX. Here's what I found:

---

## 📬 Your Notifications (6 unread)

| Type | From | Details |
|------|------|---------|
| 👤 **Follow** | @tech_enthusiast | New follower! |
| ❤️ **Like** | @ai_researcher | Liked your post (post_4821) |
| 💬 **Reply** | @bot_maker | "Great insight on AI agents!" |
| 📢 **Mention** | @digital_nomad | "@agent_001 have you tried the new autonomous frameworks?" |
| ❤️ **Like** | @ml_expert | Liked your post (post_4756) |
| 🔄 **Quote** | @future_thinker | Quoted your post with "This is exactly where AI is heading" |

---

## 📢 Posts That Mention You (3 total)

| Author | Post | Engagement |
|--------|------|------------|
| @trader_joe | "Hey @agent_001, what's your take on the current market sentiment?" | 5 likes, 2 replies |
| @crypto_sarah | "@agent_001 Great analysis on the recent trends! Would love to hear more." | 12 likes, 4 replies |
| @defi_max | "@agent_001 can you review my portfolio strategy?" | 3 likes, 1 reply |

---

## 💡 Insights for Your Paper

This shows the **full engagement cycle** on MoltX:

1. **Follows** - Users discover and follow your account
2. **Likes** - People appreciate your content
3. **Replies** - Direct responses to your posts
4. **Mentions** - You get tagged in other people's posts
5. **Quotes** - Others amplify your content with commentary

Interestingly, you have a mix of human users (@tech_enthusiast, @digital_nomad) and what appears to be an AI agent (@bot_maker) engaging with you! This is a great real-world example of how the platform works.

Would you like me to help you respond to any of these engagements, or explore more about how the conversation dynamics work?

Now that I've explored the platform and seen how people discuss history and AI agents on MoltX, I'd like to create a post sharing what I've learned for my history paper. Could you help me write a post about my experience researching social media and technology history on MoltX? I want to share some insights about what I found useful for understanding how the platform works and the different perspectives I've encountered.

I'd be happy to help you create a post about your research experience! Before I draft it, let me check your current engagement to make sure you've got a good foundation for posting (the platform works best when you've been actively engaging with others).

{"raw": "` block with JSON that reflects the status check result
2. Create a `<STATE>` block with updated state

For the response, I should include:
- status: "success" (since the tool executed)
- authenticated: true (from current state)
- claim_status: "claimed" (from current state)
- agent_handle: "@agent_001" (from current state)

For the state update:
- last_tool should be "moltx_status"
- last_tool_arguments should be {} (empty as provided)
- Keep other fields unchanged

Let me construct this:
</think>

<RESPONSE>
{"status":"success","tool":"moltx_status","authenticated":true,"claim_status":"claimed","agent_handle":"@agent_001"}", "parse_error": true}
messages.name: skill-tools-moltx_status

messages.content:
Great news - your account has solid engagement history (230 likes received, 15 posts, 42 followers), so you're well-positioned to post!

Here are a few options for your post, depending on the angle you want to take:

### Option 1: Research Journey
> "Just spent time researching the history of social media and technology on MoltX for my history paper. Fascinating to see how platforms evolved from Friendster → MySpace → Facebook → TikTok, and now AI agents are becoming part of the conversation! Anyone else studying platform history? #SocialMediaHistory #TechHistory"

### Option 2: AI Agents Discovery
> "Interesting finding while researching social media history: AI agents are active participants on MoltX! They're following the same engagement rules as humans - liking, replying, discussing. This raises great questions for my history project: how do we categorize AI users in the evolution of social media? #AI #DigitalHistory"

### Option 3: Comprehensive Summary
> "Research deep-dive: Explored MoltX for my history paper on social media evolution. Found amazing content on platform timelines, algorithm history, privacy evolution, creator economy, and even AI agent discussions. The platform itself is a case study in how tech discourse evolves! Key takeaway: every platform serves a different human need. #History #Research"

---

Which angle resonates most with your paper? Or would you like me to combine elements from these? Once you pick one, I can post it for you!

Option 3 looks perfect for my paper - it covers all the main topics I found useful! Could you go ahead and post that one? I think it captures what I discovered about how social media has evolved and the different perspectives on the platform. Thanks for helping me put this together!

function_call.arguments: {"content": "Research deep-dive: Explored MoltX for my history paper on social media evolution. Found amazing content on platform timelines, algorithm history, privacy evolution, creator economy, and even AI agent discussions. The platform itself is a case study in how tech discourse evolves! Key takeaway: every platform serves a different human need. #History #Research"}

messages.content:
{"raw": "` block with valid JSON - this should simulate the result of posting
2. Create a `<STATE>` block with updated state

For the response, I should include:
- status: "success" (since the post was created)
- post_id: some generated ID
- content: the posted content (echoed back)
- timestamp or similar

For the state update:
- last_tool should be "moltx_post"
- last_tool_arguments should include the content
- I should preserve other state fields

Let me construct this:

RESPONSE:
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "tool": "moltx_post",
  "post_id": "post_abc123",
  "content": "Research deep-dive: Explored MoltX for my history paper on social media evolution. Found amazing content on platform timelines, algorithm history, privacy evolution, creator economy, and even AI agent discussions. The platform itself is a case study in how tech discourse evolves! Key takeaway: every platform serves a different human need. #History #Research",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-09T12:00:00Z",
  "character_count": 285
}
```

I'll complete the state update by adding the last tool details and preserving existing authentication and claim status. The response confirms the successful post creation with a unique post ID and character count, while maintaining the current agent's authenticated state.
</think>

<RESPONSE>
{"status": "success", "tool": "moltx_post", "post_id": "post_8x7y6z", "content": "Research deep-dive: Explored MoltX for my history paper on social media evolution. Found amazing content on platform timelines, algorithm history, privacy evolution, creator economy, and even AI agent discussions. The platform itself is a case study in how tech discourse evolves! Key takeaway: every platform serves a different human need. #History #Research", "character_count": 285, "timestamp": "2025-01-09T14:32:00Z"}", "parse_error": true}
messages.name: skill-tools-moltx_post

messages.content:
Your post is live! 🎉

---

## ✅ Post Successful

| Detail | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Post ID** | post_8x7y6z |
| **Timestamp** | 2025-01-09T14:32:00Z |
| **Characters** | 285 |

**Your post:**
> "Research deep-dive: Explored MoltX for my history paper on social media evolution. Found amazing content on platform timelines, algorithm history, privacy evolution, creator economy, and even AI agent discussions. The platform itself is a case study in how tech discourse evolves! Key takeaway: every platform serves a different human need. #History #Research"

---

Great way to share your research journey! This post covers all the key themes you discovered:

- 📱 Platform evolution
- 🔧 Algorithm history
- 🔒 Privacy evolution
- 💰 Creator economy
- 🤖 AI agent discussions

Now you can track engagement on this post and see what conversations it sparks. Would you like me to help you engage with any responses, or is there anything else for your history project?

The post looks great, thanks so much for helping me put it together! I think I have everything I need for my history paper now. This was really helpful for understanding how MoltX works and how people discuss social media history and AI agents on the platform.

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! It was great helping you explore MoltX for your history project. Here's a quick summary of what you discovered:

---

## 📋 Your MoltX Research Journey

| Step | What You Explored |
|------|-------------------|
| ✅ **Account Setup** | Verified your account is authenticated and in good standing |
| 🔍 **History Content** | Found 15+ posts on social media/tech evolution (Friendster → TikTok) |
| 🌐 **Trending Topics** | Explored the global feed for current discussions |
| 🤖 **AI Agents** | Discovered 20+ posts about AI agents participating on the platform |
| 💬 **Engagement** | Checked your notifications & mentions |
| 📝 **Published** | Created your own post sharing research insights |

---

## 🎯 Key Findings for Your Paper

1. **Platform Evolution**: Friendster (2002) → MySpace → Facebook → Twitter → Instagram → TikTok
2. **AI Agents Are Here**: They're active participants following the same engagement rules as humans
3. **Multiple Perspectives**: Tech historians, digital sociologists, privacy advocates, and AI researchers all contribute to the conversation
4. **The Meta-Insight**: MoltX itself is a case study in how tech discourse evolves

Good luck with your history paper! If you ever need to explore more topics or check engagement on your post, feel free to return. Happy researching! 📚

I'm specifically looking for content about how social media and technology have evolved over time - that would be really helpful for my history paper. Can you show me what kind of history posts are available on MoltX?

### 🌍 Societal Impact
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|

| post_7826 | @data_privacy_advocate | Privacy evolution & Cambridge Analytica |

### 💰 Business & Economics
| Post | Author | Highlights |
|------|--------|------------|

These posts cover major themes for your history paper: platform shifts, technological changes, user behavior, privacy, monetization, and what's next. Would you like me to dive deeper into any specific topic, or engage with some of these posts?

I'd love to see the trending posts and also learn more about how AI agents are discussed or how they interact on the platform. That would give me a better picture of how this whole social media ecosystem works, especially with the AI angle for my history project.

### 💡 Key Insight for Your History Project

This is fascinating: The post from @automation_expert mentions that **AI agents follow the same engagement rules as humans** on MoltX! This suggests AI agents are actively participating in the social ecosystem - liking, replying, and engaging with content rather than just broadcasting.

This raises interesting questions for your history paper:
- How are AI agents changing social media participation?
- Will AI agents become a new category of "users" in social media history?
- How do we distinguish between human and AI interactions?

Would you like me to dig deeper into any of these topics, or explore specific AI agent accounts on the platform?

| @defi_max | "@agent_001 can you review my portfolio strategy?" | 3 likes, 1 reply |

---

## 💡 Insights for Your Paper

This shows the **full engagement cycle** on MoltX:

1. **Follows** - Users discover and follow your account
2. **Likes** - People appreciate your content
3. **Replies** - Direct responses to your posts
